78.reason: [Error: loop condition should be i < strlen(s) to avoid printing the null terminator]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: loop condition should be i < strlen(s) to avoid printing the null terminator]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  out-of-bounds read: i goes up to strlen(s), accessing s[strlen(s)] which is the null terminator, causing an extra character to be printed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `i<=strlen(s)` causes the loop to access `s[strlen(s)]`, which is the null terminator `\0`. This results in printing an extra null character or garbage at the end of the output, causing the "Wrong Answer" due to the mismatch with the expected output which ends cleanly with the exponent. It should be `i<strlen(s)`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `i<=strlen(s)` causes the loop to access `s[strlen(s)]`, which is the null terminator `\0`. While `printf` might ignore it or print nothing visible depending on implementation, strictly speaking, it processes beyond the significant digits. More importantly, in the specific test case `87899.9999999999000999`, the logic for `m>n` (number >= 10) is executed. The variable `n` is correctly identified as 0 (first non-zero digit '8'). The loop starts at `i=1`. It prints all characters except '.' and '-'. This part seems logically okay for constructing the mantissa digits. However, looking at the Buggy Output `8.78999999999999000999 e4` vs Expected `8.78999999999999000999e4`, there is no space in the expected output. The provided buggy output in the prompt description has a weird character `` which often represents a null byte or unprintable character. The loop `i<=strlen(s)` will eventually hit `s[strlen(s)]` which is `\0`. `printf("%c", '\0')` prints a null character. This null character is likely what appears as the artifact before `e4` or causes the comparison failure. The loop should be `i<strlen(s)`.
